repo_notes.content: MDM (Markdown Management) is a Go CLI for managing reusable markdown-based prompt libraries ('skills') for AI coding agents. It is intentionally a tool for *humans and CI* — not for an agent to run on itself. Treat the README and AGENTS.md as the canonical source for the public CLI surface. The CLI surface is defined entirely in commands/*.go using Cobra; main.go is a thin entry that calls commands.BuildRootCmd.

Two pieces of state matter throughout the codebase: (1) skills-lock.json — written by internal/lock/, records installed skills with their source, ref, hashes, and the project's configuredAgents list. (2) The agent registry — internal/agent/agent.go defines AllAgents, the map of ~45 supported AI tools and their per-agent skills/instruction file paths. Almost every command reads or writes one of these.

The mdm skills add install flow is the most important pipeline in the codebase. It lives in commands/installer.go and orchestrates: source parsing (internal/source) → fetch via git clone or GitHub blob API (internal/git, internal/blob) → SKILL.md discovery (internal/skill) → hidden-character scan (internal/security/markdownscan, invoked from commands/hidden_scan.go) → agent picker → copy or symlink into each agent's skills directory → record in skills-lock.json. Any docs page about installs should reference this order.

Security posture is a deliberate selling point. There are three layers: (1) Hidden-character / prompt-smuggling scan over every markdown file before install — see commands/hidden_scan.go and internal/security/markdownscan. (2) Pre-install advisory check against skills.sh and OSV — see commands/audit.go. (3) Lockfile hash verification — installs can be reproduced byte-for-byte from skills-lock.json. Do not document these as optional; the scan is mandatory and requires --allow-hidden-chars to bypass.

Agent terminology is overloaded — the docs use 'agent' to mean an AI tool (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.), never the DeepWiki / Devin sense of an autonomous code agent. AllAgents in internal/agent/agent.go fall into three categories: agents with their own skills directory (need a per-agent symlink), agents that use the shared .agents/skills directory, and agents that read AGENTS.md natively for instructions. Most rules.go and agents.go logic exists to handle these three categories correctly.

Do not invent flags or commands that are not in commands/*.go. Verified top-level commands as of this writing: skills, agents, rules, doctor, upgrade, uninstall, completion. The mdm agents command group (list / add / remove) is separate from internal/agent/ — agents is the user-facing 'configured agents' list that drives default install targets, persisted in skills-lock.json's configuredAgents field.

Build and release: GoReleaser builds binaries for Linux/macOS/Windows (x64 + arm64) on tag push (v* in .github/workflows/release.yml). The Windows binary embeds an icon via assets/mdm.ico → resource_windows.syso (generated by make syso using the goversioninfo tool dependency declared in go.mod). internal/version/version.go's 'dev' string is overridden via -ldflags at release time; never bump it for releases.

Pre-PR / CI checks are: gofmt -s, go test ./..., govulncheck, and gocyclo -over 16. All four must pass. The /go-report-card skill (see .agents/skills/) runs all four in order. Any doc page on contributing must mention all four, not just tests.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Explain what MDM is in one or two paragraphs: a Go CLI for managing markdown-based skill libraries across 45+ AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Copilot, Codex, etc.). Cover the value proposition — one source-of-truth AGENTS.md and a shared skills folder symlinked into each agent — and the project's identity statement: 'No telemetry · Fully open source'. Link out to Installation and Quick Start. Do not enumerate every flag here; defer to the per-command pages.

pages.title: Installation
pages.purpose: Document the three install paths: the curl|bash installer for macOS/Linux (install.sh), the PowerShell installer for Windows (install.ps1), and building from source (make build, make install, go install ./...). Note that both installers place the binary at ~/.local/bin/mdm by default and respect INSTALL_DIR. Mention `mdm upgrade` for self-updating once installed.

pages.title: Quick Start
pages.purpose: Walk a new user through the common end-to-end flow: (1) `mdm rules link` to unify CLAUDE.md / .cursorrules / .clinerules onto AGENTS.md, (2) `mdm agents add` to pick which AI tools to target, (3) `mdm skills add owner/repo` to install a skill bundle, (4) `mdm skills install` to restore everything from skills-lock.json on a new checkout. Each step should reference the dedicated page for that command.

pages.title: Skills Management
pages.purpose: Parent overview page for the `mdm skills` command group. Describe the lifecycle: discover (find) → install (add) → list / audit / update / remove → reproduce via lockfile (install) → publish via init. Show the full CLI tree for `mdm skills` and link each subcommand to its dedicated page. Define what a 'skill' is: a directory containing a SKILL.md with optional frontmatter, packaged in a GitHub/GitLab repo, URL, or local path.

pages.title: Installing skills (mdm skills add)
pages.purpose: Document the install pipeline implemented in commands/add.go and commands/installer.go. Cover: accepted package formats (owner/repo shorthand, full URLs, gitlab.com, git refs via #branch, local paths, well-known aliases), the seven-step install flow (fetch → discover → multi-skill picker → scope + agent prompt → hidden-character scan → copy or symlink → lockfile write), and every flag (--global/-g, --project/-p, --agent/-a, --skill/-s, --list/-l, --yes/-y, --copy, --all, --full-depth, --skip-audit, --fail-on-audit, --allow-hidden-chars). Explain how project vs global scope works and what symlink vs copy mode produces on disk.

page_notes.content: The shared install logic — sanitizeName, isPathSafe, skillNameMatches, the copy and symlink helpers — lives in commands/installer.go, not commands/add.go. commands/update.go and commands/install.go reuse this. Make that relationship explicit on the page.

The --agent and --skill flags accept BOTH `-a claude-code cursor` (space-separated, normalized by normalizeMultiFlags in commands/root.go) and `-a claude-code -a cursor` (repeated). Both forms are supported intentionally — document both.

pages.title: Listing skills (mdm skills list)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills list`. Cover output format (skill name, scope, source, ref), the --global / --project / --json flags, and how it reads from skills-lock.json rather than scanning disk. Alias: ls.

pages.title: Removing skills (mdm skills remove)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills remove`. Explain the interactive picker when no skill name is given, the per-agent cleanup it does (removes symlinks in each agent's skills directory, removes the shared content from .agents/skills, updates skills-lock.json), and the --agent / --skill / --global / --yes flags. Aliases: rm, r.

pages.title: Finding skills (mdm skills find)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills find [query]` — the registry search that queries the skills.sh public API. Describe the interactive flow (search prompt → spinner → multiselect → hand-off to mdm skills add), and the --json flag for non-interactive / CI use. Aliases: search, f, s.

pages.title: Updating skills (mdm skills update)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills update [skills...]` — re-fetches each installed skill from its recorded source+ref in skills-lock.json. Distinguish it from `mdm skills audit` (audit reports availability, update applies changes). Note the alias `check` for read-only behavior and how it interacts with hashes recorded in the lockfile.

pages.title: Auditing skills (mdm skills audit)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills audit`. Cover two modes: (1) the default mode that walks installed skills and reports update-available + advisory findings from the skills.sh API and OSV; (2) the pre-install mode via --source/-r (and optional --skill/-s) that audits a remote BEFORE installing. List every flag (--global, --project, --json, --source, --skill). Show the standard text output and the JSON schema (name, scope, source, syncStatus, audits[]). Document the sync status values (up-to-date, outdated, unknown, local, unchecked) and advisory severity levels (high, medium, low).

pages.title: Scaffolding a skill (mdm skills init)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills init [name]`. Explain what the scaffolded SKILL.md contains (frontmatter with name/description, sections for instructions and examples), where it is written (the current directory), and how this maps to the SKILL.md frontmatter spec. Note that the name argument is optional — it prompts when omitted.

pages.title: Restoring from lockfile (mdm skills install)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills install` — the CI / onboarding command that re-installs every skill recorded in skills-lock.json using the original source, ref, and configured agents. Distinguish from `mdm skills add` (manual single-skill install) and `mdm skills update` (re-fetch + update). Mention how it interacts with the lockfile hash to enforce reproducibility.

pages.title: node_modules sync (mdm skills sync)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm skills sync` — copies/symlinks skills shipped inside `node_modules/*/skills/` (the npm-published skill convention) into the configured agent skill directories. Useful when a project already pulls skills as npm dependencies and wants them surfaced to its AI tools.

pages.title: Configured agents (mdm agents)
pages.purpose: Document the top-level `mdm agents` command group. Explain why a configured agent list exists (it becomes the default install target so `mdm skills add` does not have to prompt), what the three agent categories are (shared skills dir, AGENTS.md-native, unique skills dir + unique instructions file), and how the list is stored per scope (project: skills-lock.json's configuredAgents field; global: ~/.agents/skills-lock.json). Document each subcommand (list, add, remove), their flags including --available and --json on list and --yes on remove, and how `mdm rules link` also writes to configuredAgents.

pages.title: Rules and AGENTS.md
pages.purpose: Document the `mdm rules` command group and the project's stance on agent instruction files. Explain why AGENTS.md is treated as the universal source of truth (read natively by Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Replit), and the per-tool file zoo it replaces (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, .clinerules, .roorules, .github/copilot-instructions.md, GEMINI.md, etc.). Cover the link / status / unlink subcommands and all their flags (--agent, --yes on link/unlink; --agent, --json on status). Show the file-state taxonomy (linked, real file, missing, broken).

pages.title: Project health (mdm doctor)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm doctor` — a top-level command (NOT `mdm skills doctor`). List every check it performs: missing skill directories or SKILL.md files; broken agent-side symlinks; skills modified since install (hash mismatch); markdown files too large; oversized agent instruction files; configured agents whose instruction file is unlinked; configured agents with linked rules but missing skill symlinks; missing README; large stray .md files in the project. Note the --global / --project flags.

pages.title: Self-update (mdm upgrade)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm upgrade` (aliases: update-cli, self-update). Cover the five-step flow (query GitHub /releases/latest → version compare → download platform binary + sha256sums.txt → verify SHA256 → atomic in-place replace), the --stable (default) and --beta flags (the latter opts into prereleases that the API normally excludes), the platform support matrix (Linux/macOS/Windows × x64/arm64; Windows uses a background batch script for the rename), and the manual-download fallback path.

pages.title: Removing the CLI (mdm uninstall)
pages.purpose: Document `mdm uninstall` (alias: remove-cli). It removes only the binary itself — not skills, not skills-lock.json, not agent instruction files. Note that on Windows it schedules the deletion in a background script because a running binary cannot delete itself.

pages.title: Shell completion
pages.purpose: Document `mdm completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell]` and `mdm completion install`. Explain the supported shells, how `install` auto-detects $SHELL / PSModulePath, which rc file each shell uses (~/.zshrc, ~/.config/fish/config.fish, ~/.bashrc or ~/.bash_profile on macOS, the PowerShell profile), and that the install is idempotent (it checks for an existing 'mdm completion' line before appending).

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Top-level overview of how the codebase is laid out. Show the directory tree (commands/, internal/, main.go, tools/, assets/) and one-line responsibilities for each subdirectory. Explain that commands/ holds Cobra command builders (one file per CLI command, plus installer.go and hidden_scan.go for shared logic), and internal/ holds the actual primitives (agent registry, skill discovery, source parsing, lockfile, git, blob, security scan, ui, version, update helpers). Link to the deeper pages.

pages.title: Install pipeline internals
pages.purpose: Deep-dive into the install pipeline that `mdm skills add` and `mdm skills update` share. Walk through, in order: internal/source/ (ParsedSource — GitHub shorthand, GitLab, well-known aliases, local paths, refs after #), internal/registry/ (.well-known/agent-skills standard lookup), internal/git/ (shallow clone with branch/ref handling), internal/blob/ (GitHub Trees + Blobs API as a clone-free alternative), internal/skill/ (recursive SKILL.md discovery, frontmatter parsing, --skill filter matching), and finally commands/installer.go (per-agent copy/symlink + lockfile write). Reference the data flow described in AGENTS.md.

pages.title: Agent registry (internal/agent)
pages.purpose: Document internal/agent/agent.go and the AllAgents map. Explain the AgentConfig fields (DisplayName, skill dir path(s) at project and global scope, optional DetectInstalled() probe, optional instruction file path, category flags). List the ~45 supported agents grouped by category (unique skills dir, shared .agents/skills, AGENTS.md-native, etc.). Show what 'Adding a new agent' looks like — a single entry in AllAgents.

pages.title: Hidden-character security scan
pages.purpose: Document the pre-install markdown scan implemented in internal/security/markdownscan and invoked from commands/hidden_scan.go. List the character classes detected (Unicode tag characters U+E0000-U+E007F, bidirectional controls, zero-width joiner / non-joiner / space, variation selectors, soft hyphens). Make clear it runs on EVERY install and blocks even with --yes; the only bypass is --allow-hidden-chars. Reference docs/security/hidden-character-scan.md for the full policy.

pages.title: Lockfile (skills-lock.json)
pages.purpose: Document the schema written and read by internal/lock/. Each entry records: skill name, source string, parsed source type, ref (branch / tag / commit), content hash, install timestamp, target scope. The top-level configuredAgents array records the project's default agent targets (shared with mdm agents and mdm rules link). Show a representative example. Mention that hashes are used by `mdm doctor` to detect drift and by `mdm skills install` to enforce reproducibility.

pages.title: SKILL.md spec
pages.purpose: Document the SKILL.md file format that this project produces (via `mdm skills init`) and consumes (during install discovery). Cover the YAML frontmatter fields (name, description, optional tags, optional license), the markdown body conventions (Instructions, Examples), and how internal/skill/ parses and filters them. Explain how the --skill flag matches against the frontmatter name field.

pages.title: Adding a new agent
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for contributors: edit internal/agent/agent.go, append an AgentConfig entry to AllAgents with the agent's display name, per-scope skills directory paths, optional instruction file path, optional DetectInstalled callback. Note that no other file needs to change — the picker, completion, and install logic all read from AllAgents.

pages.title: Adding a new command
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for contributors: create a new file in commands/ defining a `cobra.Command` builder (`buildFooCmd()`), then register it on the appropriate parent — root for top-level commands (in commands/root.go's BuildRootCmd) or on the skills group (in commands/skills.go) for skill subcommands. Reference existing examples: commands/doctor.go (root-level, no args), commands/find.go (skills-level, optional query), commands/agents.go (group with its own subcommands).

pages.title: Build, test, and the Windows icon
pages.purpose: Cover the developer build loop: make build, make test, make install, go run . Then document Windows packaging — assets/mdm.svg → tools/gen-icon/ → assets/mdm.ico (committed, multi-resolution 16/32/48/64/128/256), assets/versioninfo.json, resource_windows.syso generated by `make syso` via the goversioninfo tool dependency in go.mod. Explain how the _windows suffix on the .syso file is a Go build constraint so it only links into Windows targets.

pages.title: Release process and Pre-PR checklist
pages.purpose: Document the release flow and the contributor pre-PR checklist. Release: tag v* and push — .github/workflows/release.yml runs GoReleaser, which builds Linux/macOS/Windows (x64+arm64) and publishes a GitHub release with the tag injected via ldflags. Prereleases (v*-rc.*) are marked prerelease and skipped by `mdm upgrade` unless --beta is passed. Pre-PR: run `/go-report-card` (or manually: gofmt -s, go test ./..., govulncheck, gocyclo -over 16). All four must pass; CI gates merge on them.
